title: Opportunity
text:
Students interested in undergraduate projects, master’s dissertations, internships, or future Ph.D. opportunities are encouraged to contact me by email. 




OPEN · M.TECH INTERNSHIP





Research internship in physics-informed machine learning for industrial applications, with a focus on process evolution modelling and soft sensor development using sparse industrial time-series data.



Duration 6 months

Possibility of extension up to one year



Stipend ₹40,000 / month

Industry research internship



Location Bengaluru 


Eligibility M.Tech students

Students from any discipline may apply






Physics-Informed Neural Networks Neural Operators Machine Learning Deep Learning Industrial Time-Series Soft Sensors




Students with knowledge of differential equations and machine learning, particularly those interested in physics-informed machine learning.


Interested
Send me your CV and a short note describing your background in differential equations and machine learning.
 Email me about this opportunity → 















Scientific Machine Learning


Physics-Informed Neural Networks


Neural Differential Equations


Fractional Calculus


Dynamical Systems


Control Theory


Numerical Analysis


Scientific Computing


Applied Mathematics













While at NIT Warangal as Assistant Professor

title: About
text:
I am an Assistant Professor of Mathematics at the National Institute of Technology Warangal. My research lies at the intersection of Scientific Machine Learning, Dynamical Systems, and Control Theory.
I develop mathematically rigorous learning and computational methods for problems arising in differential equations, dynamical systems, scientific computing, and control.

Prior to joining NIT Warangal, I was a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Laboratoire de Mécanique Paris-Saclay, École Normale Supérieure Paris-Saclay, Université Paris-Saclay, France, where I worked under the supervision of Prof. Ludovic Chamoin. My postdoctoral research focused on learning-based Model Predictive Control, neural networks, and Scientific Machine Learning.

text:
Assistant Professor

Department of Mathematics
National Institute of Technology Warangal

Warangal, Telangana, India



Scientific Machine Learning · Dynamical Systems · Control Theory


Email (siva915544 at gmail dot com)
GitHub
Google Scholar
ORCID
ResearchGate





MATHEMATICS · SCIENTIFIC MACHINE LEARNING



My research lies at the intersection of Scientific Machine Learning and Dynamical Systems, with particular emphasis on the mathematical analysis and computational development of learning-based methods for differential equations and control.
My work spans neural differential equations, physics-informed learning, control theory, fractional-order systems, and data-driven numerical methods.

title: Projects
text:
My research spans mathematical foundations, scientific machine learning, dynamical systems, and learning-based control. 











01




Mathematical foundations and computational methods combining scientific models with machine learning.





02




Continuous-depth neural networks, neural ODEs, and learning-based dynamical systems.





03




Physics-informed and theory-informed learning methods for differential equations.





04




Learning-based control, nonlinear systems, stability, and systems under uncertainty.





05




Robust MPC, Tube MPC, and learning-based control policies.





06




Fractional calculus, fractional differential equations, and fractional dynamics.





07




Neural operators and learning mappings between infinite-dimensional function spaces.





08




Numerical analysis, stochastic differential equations, optimization, and computational mathematics.













Researchers interested in collaboration in any of the above areas are welcome to contact me.

 Contact me →
